changes.value:
## What

Add an opt-in `groupable` boolean to field definitions so the UI can filter which fields appear in the group-by selector. This card covers the backend: Rust struct and YAML field definition files.

### Files to modify

- `swissarmyhammer-fields/src/types.rs` ‚Äî add `pub groupable: Option<bool>` to `FieldDef` struct (line ~136) with `#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]`
- YAML field definitions in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/fields/definitions/` ‚Äî add `groupable: true` to categorical fields:
  - `position_column.yaml` (status ‚Äî primary grouping use case)
  - `position_swimlane.yaml`
  - `tags.yaml`
  - `assignees.yaml`
  - `color.yaml`
  - `tag_name.yaml`
- Leave all other fields without `groupable` (defaults to `None`/false ‚Äî title, body, description, attachments, progress, etc.)

### Design decision

Opt-in (`groupable: true` required) rather than opt-out. This is safer ‚Äî new fields are ungroupable by default, and only fields explicitly marked appear in the selector.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `FieldDef` Rust struct has `groupable: Option<bool>` that round-trips through serde YAML and JSON
- [ ] `get_entity_schema` Tauri command serializes `groupable` in the JSON response
- [ ] The 6 categorical field YAML files have `groupable: true`
- [ ] All other field YAML files do NOT have `groupable`
- [ ] Existing tests pass ‚Äî no deserialization breakage from the new optional field

## Tests

- [ ] Add unit test in `swissarmyhammer-fields/src/types.rs` (or nearby test module): deserialize a YAML snippet with `groupable: true`, assert the field is `Some(true)`. Deserialize without it, assert `None`.
- [ ] Run: `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-fields` ‚Äî all tests pass
- [ ] Run: `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` ‚Äî all tests pass (entity loading with new field)

## Workflow

- Use `/tdd` ‚Äî write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
Add `groupable` attribute to FieldDef (Rust + YAML definitions)

--- original

@@ -1,29 +1,29 @@
 ## What

-Add an opt-in `groupable` boolean to field definitions so the UI can filter which fields appear in the group-by selector. This card covers the backend: Rust struct and YAML field definition files.
+Add an opt-in `groupable` boolean to field definitions so the UI can filter which fields appear in the group-by selector. This card covers the backend: Rust struct and YAML field definition files. Also fix the stale Rust doc comment that says `group` is a "JS expression" ‚Äî it's a plain field name.

 ### Files to modify

 - `swissarmyhammer-fields/src/types.rs` ‚Äî add `pub groupable: Option<bool>` to `FieldDef` struct (line ~136) with `#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]`
+- `swissarmyhammer-perspectives/src/types.rs` ‚Äî update doc comment on `Perspective.group` from "Opaque group function string (JS expression). Stored, not evaluated." to "Group-by field name. Stored as a plain field name string, consumed by the UI as a TanStack Table grouping column ID."
 - YAML field definitions in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/fields/definitions/` ‚Äî add `groupable: true` to categorical fields:
-  - `position_column.yaml` (status ‚Äî primary grouping use case)
-  - `position_swimlane.yaml`
   - `tags.yaml`
   - `assignees.yaml`
   - `color.yaml`
   - `tag_name.yaml`
-- Leave all other fields without `groupable` (defaults to `None`/false ‚Äî title, body, description, attachments, progress, etc.)
+- Leave all other fields without `groupable` (defaults to `None`/false ‚Äî title, body, description, position_column, position_swimlane, position_ordinal, attachments, progress, etc.)

 ### Design decision

-Opt-in (`groupable: true` required) rather than opt-out. This is safer ‚Äî new fields are ungroupable by default, and only fields explicitly marked appear in the selector.
+Opt-in (`groupable: true` required) rather than opt-out. This is safer ‚Äî new fields are ungroupable by default, and only fields explicitly marked appear in the selector. Position fields (column, swimlane) are NOT groupable ‚Äî those represent board layout, not meaningful categorical data for grid grouping.

 ## Acceptance Criteria

 - [ ] `FieldDef` Rust struct has `groupable: Option<bool>` that round-trips through serde YAML and JSON
 - [ ] `get_entity_schema` Tauri command serializes `groupable` in the JSON response
-- [ ] The 6 categorical field YAML files have `groupable: true`
-- [ ] All other field YAML files do NOT have `groupable`
+- [ ] The 4 categorical field YAML files have `groupable: true`
+- [ ] position_column, position_swimlane, and all other field YAML files do NOT have `groupable`
+- [ ] Rust doc comment on `Perspective.group` reflects reality (plain field name, not JS expression)
 - [ ] Existing tests pass ‚Äî no deserialization breakage from the new optional field

 ## Tests
